evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes and remains constant year-round, making it ideal for international coordination and technical applications #### Common Conversion Examples Business Hours (Winter) EST 9:00 AM → UTC 2:00 PM EST 5:00 PM → UTC 10:00 PM 5-hour difference during winter Business Hours (Summer) EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM [...] EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to ge...

evidences.title: Binace API, Python and Close Price : r/quant - Reddit
evidences.content: # Binace API, Python and Close Price Hi guys! I need help understanding how closing prices work in real-time. In context: I use the Binance API in Python, analyzing closing prices in 30-minute windows, for this, I work with 1-minute intervals. I have a "while True" in my code that is constantly requesting through the "futures\_continous\_klines" module, limit windows of 30. When I'm running the code in real-time, the last value of the closing price always fluctuates according to each loop do while (which was to be expected). [...] In my Backtests, when I work with historical data, I have perfect closing prices every 1 minute. I would like to know if there is a possibility of obtaining the last value of the window without fluctuation and closing at the perfect clock minute. In my tests, I decided to set a sleep time of 60 seconds, using the clock as a reference, however, my graphs always "appeared" out of place compared to historical data. Does anyone have any suggestions on how I can get around this problem? I want my real-time closing price values to be the same as the closing price values in the historical data I use. Thank you very much! # Related Answers Section Create your...

evidences.title: EST to UTC
evidences.content: Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. Copied embed code to clipboard! ## Frequently Asked Questions ### How do you convert Eastern Time to UTC? UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST or 4 hours ahead of EDT ### What is the time difference between Eastern Time and UTC? The time difference between Eastern Time and Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) depends on whether Daylight Saving Time is in effect. During standard time (EST), UTC is 5 hours ahead. During daylight time (EDT), UTC is 4 hours ahead. evidences.content: ### What are the national holidays in the EST time zone? National holidays: New Year's Day: Celebrated on January 1st, marking the start of the new year. Independence Day: Observed on July 4th, with parades, fireworks, and celebrations throughout the region. Labor Day: Celebrated on the first Monday in September, honoring American workers and their contributions. Thanksgiving Day: Observed on the fourth Thursday in November, a major holiday for family gatherings and feasting. Christmas Day: Celebrated on December 25th, marked by family gatherings, feasts, and festive celebrations. ### What is some local trivia or geography about the EST region? [...] ### What is the difference between EST and EDT? EDT (Eastern Daylight Time) is used during Daylight Saving time and is UTC-4 hours. EST (Eastern Standard Time) is used outside of Daylight Saving Time and is UTC-5 hours. ### What are some major industries in the EST time zone?
